top_categories.workout_context: 14,436ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Les Cinglés du Mont Ventoux's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
At mile 60 of 85, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
170ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Les Cinglés du Mont Ventoux. Train it until it feels boring.
Les Cinglés du Mont Ventoux's paved — three distinct ascents of Mont Ventoux forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 85 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 85 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At Les Cinglés du Mont Ventoux, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 14,436ft of climbing on paved — three distinct ascents of Mont Ventoux does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 85 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved — three distinct ascents of Mont Ventoux at tempo for 85 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Tempo is Les Cinglés du Mont Ventoux's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 85 miles on paved — three distinct ascents of Mont Ventoux. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Les Cinglés du Mont Ventoux’s 85 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Les Cinglés du Mont Ventoux. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 85 miles of paved — three distinct ascents of mont ventoux in Bédoin, Vaucluse, Provence, France.
